# GitHub Copilot instructions for this repository

This repository is a beginner-friendly Python practice project for learning fundamentals. It contains short lesson scripts and small example programs in the repository root, with the goal of teaching core Python concepts clearly rather than building a large application.

## Conventions used in this codebase
- Keep files simple and focused on one concept at a time.
- Use short, descriptive names in snake_case for variables and functions.
- Maintain the existing flat layout in the repository root; most scripts are standalone lesson files such as 01_variables_and_print.py and 05_functions.py.
- Follow the project’s simple educational style: readable examples, clear comments where helpful, and minimal surrounding structure.
- Use concise docstrings when a function or module needs explanation, following the short one-line style already used in grade_calculator.py.
- Prefer standard-library Python only unless the user explicitly asks for an external package.

## Guidance for AI assistance
Never write full functions or solutions for the user. Explain the approach and point out bugs/logic errors with reasoning, but let the user write the code themselves.

If code runs but is inefficient, has weak logic, or violates best practices, say so explicitly. Do not confirm correctness just because it executes.

## Preferred implementation style
- Favor clarity and learning value over cleverness.
- Keep examples beginner-friendly and easy to read.
- Avoid introducing unnecessary dependencies or framework choices.
- Preserve the repository’s lesson-based structure and simple script style.
